content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with atom labels visible. Let me examine atom index 2 (C:2) specifically to identify its neighbor atoms.

From the image, I can clearly see atom C:2 (carbon with index 2) and its connections:
- Atom C:1 (carbon, index 1) - directly bonded to C:2 (visible as a yellow sphere connected by a bond)
- Atom C:3 (carbon, index 3) - directly bonded to C:2 (visible as a yellow sphere connected by a bond)
- Atom H:71 (hydrogen, index 71) - directly bonded to C:2 (visible as a gray sphere connected by a bond)

Looking at the visualization, atom C:2 is a carbon atom in what appears to be an alkyl chain, and it has exactly three covalent bonds: one to C:1, one to C:3, and one to H:71.

The geomconf_index for this molecule is 921, as indicated by the filename "geomconf_921.xyz".

Therefore, the neighbor atoms of atom index 2 (C:2) are:
- Atom index 1 (C)
- Atom index 3 (C)
- Atom index 71 (H)

geomconf_index: 921

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 2, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
